flavor_text: A ragged cap cut from hide too thin for winter hunts.
This mask was stitched by lantern light and bad luck.
Rain warped the brim, but it still breaks a silhouette.
A plain hood that smells of moss, sweat, and bowstring wax.
Frayed cowl seams scratch the ears through long watches.
Scout leather hooded for thorn thickets and low branches.
Its cheek wrap is dark with marsh water and old blood.
A trail mask worn by outriders who sleep in reeds.
The brow tie is knotted with wolf hair for luck.
Field headwrap that keeps sight clear in driving sleet.
Old arrow nicks scar the ridge above the left eye.
A ranger cowl meant for moonlit stalking, not honor duels.
The inner lining muffles breath to a patient hush.
Weathered leather turns blade glints into dull shadows.
Worn by hunt crews that tracked raiders through peat fog.
Its hood peak bends low against ashfall winds.
A wayfarer mask that vanishes among dead pines.
Stitched charms along the seam rattle before ambush.
This visor watched caravans disappear beyond black fen.
Night scouts favored it when torches drew too much death.
The face wrap carries the bitter scent of crow feathers.
A kingshunt cowl for arrows loosed without warning calls.
Its shadow line hides the eyes until the strike lands.
Veteran trackers wore this hood into haunted tree lines.
An ascendant mask woven for hunts beneath a dying moon.
A thin tunic patched with hide from starving deer.
Its laces fray where the quiver rubs all day.
Plain leathers creak softly like wet bark under strain.
This jerkin keeps rain out better than knives.
Rough straps cross the chest in hurried field repairs.
Scout jerkin cut close for sprinting through thorn breaks.
Layered hide spreads arrow shock across the ribs.
Field leathers worn smooth by saddle and ash wind.
A ranger harness built for climbing ruined watchtowers.
Its collar sits low to clear longbow draw.
The chest wrap is stained by pine resin and blood.
Huntbound leathers made for raids before first light.
Dark seams swallow moonlight in open marsh.
Reinforced shoulders keep the draw arm steady at full pull.
This trail harness has outlived three commanders and two banners.
A wayfarer vest stitched with map marks of forbidden paths.
The inner weave dries fast after river crossings in armor.
Shadow leathers for hunters who return alone.
Scars on the breast tell of boar tusks and spear points.
Night raiment tuned for silent movement through ruined courts.
Its hide plates flex like muscle under hard turns.
A vanguard harness worn in ambush lines and retreat fires.
The dark grain drinks torchlight until the body disappears.
Veteran rangers called this coat the widowmaker.
Ascendant leathers fit for kingslayer trails and starless hunts.
Small shoulder pads tied with sinew and twine.
One guard sits crooked from an old branch fall.
Frayed wraps cushion the collarbone from bow recoil.
Plain shoulders that snag brush and tear quietly.
Rough hide caps keep briars off the neck.
Scout shoulders cut narrow for crawling through roots.
Their edges are dark from soot and pine pitch.
Trail guards built to turn glancing hatchet blows.
The left wrap carries teeth marks from winter wolves.
Field mantle keeps arrows from chafing the draw shoulder.
Old stitchwork binds the seam where iron split leather.
Hunt pauldron pair favored by marsh skirmishers.
These guards flex without creaking under sudden pivots.
Reinforced wraps deflect branch strikes at full run.
Worn by outriders who fought from saddle to mud.
Wayfarer mantles shed rain like blackened leaves.
Their silhouette breaks the human line in moonlight.
Shadow shoulders stitched with crow-quill charms.
Every nick marks a narrow escape in the thornlands.
Night mantles for scouts sent beyond horn range.
The right guard is lacquered with swamp tar.
Vanguard shoulders hardened for melee after empty quivers.
They sit light, then lock hard when steel arrives.
Veterans passed these wraps to only one apprentice.
Ascendant pauldrons meant for hunts where even priests refuse.
Ragged gloves with split fingertips and cold palms.
These wraps were sewn from scraps of saddle leather.
Frayed cuffs drink rain and dry by body heat.
Plain gloves leave black wax on every string.
Rough seams bite the thumb at full draw.
Scout gloves shaped for quick nock and release.
Their grip leather is scarred by barbed shafts.
Trail wraps keep fingers alive in frost dawns.
A ranger pair balanced between bowwork and knifework.
Field gloves with knuckles hardened by boiled hide.
Old blood sits dark in the stitching valleys.
Hunt wraps built for hours behind wet stone.
The wrist cords tighten when hands start shaking.
Reinforced palms hold steady through recoil and panic.
Worn by sentries who counted breaths before shooting.
Wayfarer gloves dry fast after river ambushes.
Their grip teeth catch steel when blades get close.
Shadow wraps lined with thin ash cloth.
These hands were made for killing without sound.
Night gloves favored by trackers of outlaw courts.
The inner seams carry splinters from snapped bows.
Vanguard grips for last-ditch fights in muddy alleys.
They hold like claws when blood slicks the hilt.
Veterans call this pair the final warning.
Ascendant gloves fitted for arrows, throats, and mercy denied.
A thin belt knotted with cracked cord and brass wire.
Its buckle was cast from a broken trap jaw.
Worn straps creak like old branches in wind.
Plain sash holds pouches and little else.
Frayed cord bites the waist on steep climbs.
Scout belt set with loops for spare strings.
Trail cinch keeps knives from slapping at a run.
Field girdle darkened by marsh water and smoke.
A ranger sash marked with tally cuts for confirmed kills.
Its clasp locks fast when sprinting downhill.
The leather smells of resin, rain, and wolf fat.
Hunt girdle built for long stalks without campfires.
Small hooks carry darts dipped in bitter toxins.
Reinforced waistband steadies hips for rapid turns.
Worn by outriders who rode through burning farms.
Wayfarer belt threaded with map charms and teeth.
Its knotwork hides a slim blade for close silence.
Shadow cinch that keeps gear quiet in brush.
This sash was taken from a dead border captain.
Night girdle tuned for movement through collapsed halls.
The buckle sigil was filed off after mutiny.
Vanguard belt for those who fight after arrows run out.
It rides low like a hunter ready to pounce.
Veterans buried these belts with their dogs.
Ascendant sash bound for the last hunt before dawn.
Thin leggings patched with hide from lean winters.
Their knees shine smooth from crawling stone.
Frayed trouser seams leak cold swamp water.
Plain legwraps snag on roots and barbed wire.
Rough leather strips bind old tendon pain.
Scout leggings cut for speed over distance.
Trail leathers flex cleanly through steep descents.
Field trousers reinforced where briars tear deepest.
A ranger set made for long marches without roads.
Their thigh guards turn cane blades and wolf teeth.
Dark mud stains never left these knees.
Hunt leathers favored in boar pits and fog marsh.
The hip seams hold under sudden side steps.
Reinforced wraps keep stride true on broken stairs.
Worn by patrols that vanished past the dead mill.
Wayfarer leggings stitched with route marks and warnings.
These legs were trained for pursuit through rain ash.
Shadow trousers for ambushes in half-flooded streets.
Every cut line remembers a close escape.
Night leathers tuned for fast turns in tight ruins.
The inner padding is thin, deliberate, and cruel.
Vanguard legwear for the run between cover and killing.
They move like silence until steel starts talking.
Veterans called these the grave-walkers.
Ascendant leggings for hunts where tracks end in prayer.
Ragged boots with thin soles and stubborn laces.
Worn shoes patched with bark leather and pitch.
Mud-caked treads whisper across rotten planks.
Plain boots that blister first, then harden the feet.
Frayed tongues keep out little but dust.
Scout boots built for scrub hills and wet ravines.
Trail treads bite root and shale without complaint.
Field boots dark with bog water and candle soot.
A ranger pair worn smooth by relentless pursuit.
Their heels land soft, then push hard.
Old cuts along the toe caps show knife work.
Hunt boots favored by trackers of man and beast.
The sole stitch holds through long river crossings.
Reinforced ankles survive sudden pivots in loose gravel.
Worn by outriders who rode through arrow storms.
Wayfarer boots marked by frost and old embers.
These treads keep balance on parapets slick with rain.
Shadow boots that leave little sign in ash.
Each step feels measured, merciless, and patient.
Night boots for raids launched under dead bells.
The left heel hides a needle shard.
Vanguard treads made for charges after midnight.
They run quiet until the kill is certain.
Veterans laced these before speaking last rites.
Ascendant boots meant for moonless hunts and royal blood.
